rationale: Mirror the insert/`if H { ... }` success pattern: the ensures `H ==> det_equal` is an implication, so the per-run conjuncts aren't free hypotheses — we re-state H1 && H2 as an explicit `if` path condition (binding `r1`/`r2` to fresh `rr_i`/`ne_i` names) and, inside the branch, assert each per-run ensures-conjunct plus the cross-run consequences (`my_end_point` pivot, ok/Fail and Timeout/Packet discriminant propagation, and pointwise dst@ / Receive agreement) so z3 has aligned trigger terms for both runs when discharging `det_receive_with_demarshal_equal`.
